title: 10 best herbal creams for angina

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with angina because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that have been shown to improve cardiovascular health. The extracts from the maidenhair tree, Adiantum spp., have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat heart-related conditions, including angina. These creams help to dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing blood pressure, which can alleviate symptoms of angina. Additionally, they may also possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidants can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, which may contribute to its potential benefits for cardiovascular health.
Vasodilatory effects: Ginkgo biloba has been shown to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which could potentially help alleviate symptoms of angina by increasing oxygen delivery to the heart.
Antiplatelet properties: Ginkgo biloba may inhibit platelet aggregation, which could reduce the risk of blood clots forming in blood vessels and blocking coronary arteries.
remedy_parts: Roots: Maidenhair tree roots contain bioactive compounds that help relax blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing symptoms of angina.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree have been traditionally used to treat cardiovascular issues, including angina, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves measure out 100g of dried leaves.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Combine the ground ginkgo biloba powder with 200ml of sweet almond oil and 10ml of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat stirring occasionally until the beeswax melts completely after 20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar allowing it to cool for at least 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lime creams helps with angina because it contains potent herbal extracts that work together to relax constricted blood vessels, improving blood flow to the heart. The antispasmodic properties of these herbs help to reduce muscle spasms in the coronary arteries, allowing more oxygen-rich blood to reach the heart and surrounding tissues. As a result, individuals with angina may experience reduced frequency and severity of chest pain, improved exercise tolerance, and enhanced overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Ginkgo biloba: May help improve blood flow to the heart by dilating blood vessels, reducing the frequency and severity of anginal episodes. (Source: European Heart Journal)

Feverfew (_Tanacetum parthenium_): Possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, alleviating chest pain associated with angina. (Source: Alternative Medicine Review)

Hawthorn (_Crataegus monogyna_): Has been traditionally used to treat cardiovascular diseases, including angina, by improving blood flow, lowering blood pressure, and reducing the risk of cardiac arrhythmias. (Source: Journal of Ethnopharmacology)
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lime plant contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, making them effective in relieving angina symptoms.
Barks: The barks of the lime plant have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce cardiac stress and alleviate angina pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried flowers of tilia x europaea at dawn for optimal potency and quality.
Combine 250 grams of dried flowers with 500 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a dark glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a warm place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean dark glass jar and discard solids.
Allow the infused oil to mature at room temperature for 30 days before whipping it with 20 grams of beeswax to create a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams helps with angina because they contain hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to improve blood flow to the heart. By increasing circulation, it reduces the frequency and severity of angina attacks by allowing oxygen-rich blood to reach the heart muscle more efficiently. Additionally, St John's Wort has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, further alleviating symptoms of angina. This natural approach can be especially beneficial for those looking for an alternative or complementary treatment option.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: Hyperforin, a constituent of St. John's Wort, has been shown to have vasodilatory effects, which can help to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart, reducing the frequency and severity of angina attacks.

Hypotensive properties: St. John's Wort creams also possess hypotensive properties, meaning they can help to lower blood pressure, which is often elevated in individuals with angina, and reduce the risk of cardiac events such as heart attack or stroke.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects: The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of St. John's Wort creams may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system, both of which are thought to contribute to the development of angina and other cardiovascular conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St John's Wort contain a high concentration of hypericin, which helps to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the heart, alleviating symptoms of angina.

Buds: The buds of St John's Wort are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant properties that help to protect against oxidative stress and cardiovascular damage associated with angina.
remedy_recipe: Gather 200g of dried hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers, ensuring they are free from contaminants.
Combine the herb mixture with 100ml of high-proof ethanol in a clean glass jar, seal it tightly, and allow it to macerate for 14 days.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, squeezing the solids to extract as much essence as possible.
Mix the resulting tincture with 200g of beeswax, 100ml of sweet almond oil, and 20 drops of vitamin e oil in a double boiler, heating until the wax melts completely.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized glass jars, allowing it to set for at least 24 hours before use or storage.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Corn Silk has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the blood vessels, which can contribute to angina.

Cardiovascular protective effects: The flavonoids present in Corn Silk may help protect against cardiovascular disease by improving blood vessel function, reducing blood pressure, and preventing platelet aggregation, all of which can help alleviate angina symptoms.
Antioxidant properties: Corn Silk's antioxidant properties may help neutralize free radicals that can damage blood vessels and contribute to angina.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dog holly plant contain bioactive compounds like alkaloids and glycosides, which have been traditionally used in herbal remedies to treat cardiovascular disorders, including angina.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of dog holly are rich in saponins, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate symptoms of angina.
Barks: The barks of the plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess cardioprotective effects and may be useful in reducing anginal symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather required herbs ruscus aculeatus and other ingredients including coconut oil beeswax vitamin e oil.
Measure 200 grams of dried ruscus aculeatus leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar.
Combine 100 grams of coconut oil with 20 grams of beeswax in a small saucepan over low heat stirring occasionally.
Add the ground ruscus aculeatus to the mixture in the saucepan and stir well then remove from heat.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized glass jar and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes or until it solidifies.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dog holly creams helps with angina because they contain compounds that help to relax blood vessels, improving blood flow to the heart. The herb's flavonoids and terpenes work together to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system. This natural combination helps to alleviate chest pain and discomfort associated with angina attacks, allowing dogs to live a more comfortable life. Regular use of these creams can also help to prevent future episodes of angina.

Hawthorn creams helps with angina because they contain bioactive compounds that have a positive impact on cardiovascular health. The creams work by improving blood flow to the heart, reducing inflammation in the blood vessels, and strengthening the heart's ability to pump blood effectively. This can help alleviate symptoms of angina such as chest pain and shortness of breath. remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried crataegus monogyna leaves and 10g of beeswax, then grind them together into a fine powder.
Combine 50ml of olive oil with 30ml of distilled water in a double boiler, heating the mixture over medium heat for 5 minutes.
Add 20g of the powdered crataegus monogyna leaves and 10g of grated ginger to the warm oil mixture, stirring constantly for 1 minute.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature, then blend it in a food processor until smooth and creamy, taking approximately 2 minutes.
Transfer the cream to an airtight container, storing it in the refrigerator at a temperature of 4°c for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horse chestnut creams helps with angina because they contain aescin, a bioactive compound that has been shown to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the body. By increasing blood flow to the heart, these creams can help alleviate symptoms of angina, such as chest pain and shortness of breath. remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 100g of freshly harvested hawthorn berries (crataegus monogyna) for their flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins.
Grind 50g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for its anti-inflammatory properties.
Infuse 200ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil with the hawthorn berry powder and ginkgo leaf powder by mixing them in a glass jar and letting it sit for 4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids, and add 20g of beeswax to create a smooth consistency.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for approximately 30 minutes before whipping it into a creamy texture with a hand whisk.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried vitis vinifera leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.

Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the ingredients melt together smoothly.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool to around 60°c before adding 5ml of lavender essential oil and 2.5g of calendula flowers.
Stir the mixture well and pour it into sterilized containers for storage and use within 6 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried valeriana officinalis roots weighing approximately 20 grams for each 100 milliliters of cream base preparation time takes one hour.
Chop the harvested valeriana officinalis roots into small pieces and steep them in boiling water for 30 minutes to extract active compounds steeping time is 30 minutes.
Mix the extracted root infusion with a combination of beeswax, coconut oil, and shea butter at a ratio of 1 part infusion to 2 parts carrier oils mixing time takes 10 minutes.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over simmering water for 15 minutes to melt the beeswax and mix well heating time is 15 minutes.
Pour the warm cream base into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool completely before use cooling time takes two hours.

Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool completely before transferring it to sterile glass jars with tight-fitting lids for storage at room temperature.

Combine the dried flowers with 50 grams of cold-pressed coconut oil in a double boiler, heating gently over medium heat.
Steep the mixture for 20 minutes, allowing the lavandula angustifolia extract to infuse into the coconut oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve, discarding solids and transferring infused oil to glass containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with angina because they contain potent antioxidants, such as flavonoids and vitamins A, C, and E, which help to improve blood flow to the heart. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, alleviating chest pain and discomfort associated with angina. Additionally, dandelion's diuretic effects help to lower blood pressure and alleviate fluid retention, further contributing to its beneficial effects on cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Cardiac glycosides: Dandelion cream contains cardiac glycosides, which can help regulate heart rhythm and reduce symptoms of angina by improving blood flow to the heart.

Inulin: Inulin, a type of fiber found in dandelion roots, has been shown to help lower cholesterol levels and improve blood lipid profiles, reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease and alleviating anginal symptoms.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in dandelion cream, such as sesquiterpenes, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system, contributing to a reduction in angina symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the heart, alleviating angina symptoms.
Buds: Containing salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to thin the blood and prevent platelet aggregation, reducing the risk of cardiovascular events.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 20 grams of dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers.
Combine weighed ingredients with 100 milliliters of carrier oil in double boiler.
Heat mixture under low heat for 30 minutes, then remove from heat and steep for 4 hours.
Strain infused oil through cheesecloth and discard solids, retaining 50 milliliters of infused liquid.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax with 40 milliliters of petroleum jelly, adding infused liquid and stirring until smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow gentian creams helps with angina because they contain the herb Gentiana lutea, which has been traditionally used to improve circulation and reduce inflammation. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to relax blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and improving oxygen flow to the heart. Additionally, yellow gentian's vasodilatory effects can help to dilate coronary arteries, increasing blood flow to areas of the heart affected by angina. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate symptoms of angina, providing relief from chest pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Cardiac glycosides: Gentiana lutea contains cardenolides, which can help regulate heart rhythm and increase cardiac contractility, potentially reducing symptoms of angina.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The plant's flavonoids and terpenoids have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart, alleviating anginal pain.

Vasodilatory effects: Gentiana lutea has been shown to cause vasodilation (widening of blood vessels), which can help reduce blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of angina by increasing blood flow to the heart.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the yellow gentian plant contain flavonoids and alkaloids that help to dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow, making them effective in reducing symptoms of angina.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the yellow gentian plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which can contribute to the development of angina.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried gentiana lutea roots in sufficient quantity for the desired quantity of cream.
Grind 50 grams of dried gentiana lutea roots into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 20 grams of ground gentiana lutea with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a glass jar.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the oil reaches 60 degrees celsius.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and let it cool to room temperature before storing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Soapwort creams helps with angina because they contain saponin, a natural foaming agent that can help to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation. This increase in blood flow helps to reduce the frequency and severity of angina episodes by allowing more oxygen-rich blood to reach the heart muscle. Additionally, soapwort's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to reduce inflammation and scar tissue formation in the coronary arteries, further contributing to its beneficial effects on angina symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Soapwort's saponin content has been shown to reduce inflammation, which is a common underlying factor in many cardiovascular conditions, including angina.
Antioxidant activity: The plant's antioxidants may help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of heart disease.
Cardiotonic properties: Soapwort has been traditionally used to support cardiac function and improve circulation, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness for this purpose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to reduce blood pressure and improve circulation, alleviating symptoms of angina.
Barks: Containing flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce pain and discomfort associated with angina.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh saponaria officinalis leaves measure 20 grams of leaves and clean them thoroughly.
Infuse the leaves in 100 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil for six hours steeping time.
Strain the infusion using cheesecloth or a coffee filter squeeze out excess oil without pressing.
Mix 50 grams of beeswax with 30 milliliters of coconut oil and 20 milliliters of infused oil heat gently stirring occasionally.
Pour the mixture into clean glass jars filling to the top let cool at room temperature.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for angina are a natural, topical alternative to traditional pharmaceutical treatments. These creams contain plant-based ingredients that help to dilate blood vessels, improve circulation, and reduce inflammation in the body. Examples of herbal creams that have been found to be effective in alleviating angina symptoms include those containing Ginkgo biloba, garlic oil, and turmeric extract. By using these creams, individuals with angina can experience improved chest pain relief, increased mobility, and enhanced quality of life, allowing them to engage in activities they previously avoided due to their condition.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat angina with herbal creams is by using a blend of herbs that have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and vasodilatory properties. Guggul, garlic, and ginger are some effective ingredients that help reduce chest pain and improve blood flow. Apply the cream topically to the affected area and massage gently for 2-3 minutes to increase circulation. Consistency is key, so use the cream regularly as directed to experience optimal benefits.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to angina that are treated with herbal creams are peripheral artery disease, Raynaud's phenomenon, and intermittent claudication. Herbal creams containing ingredients like ginkgo biloba, grape seed extract, and capsaicin may help improve blood flow, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms such as pain, numbness, and tingling in the affected areas. These creams can be used topically to provide relief from discomfort and promote overall circulation health.
